best_point.rules_reasoning:
Best-ranked site P003 for Corn scores 44.7% (fair), based on matching weather and soil signals to crop-specific ideal and limit ranges. Confidence reflects how many inputs were present (about 82% here).

What looks strong: Soil pH (7.14) is within the ideal band (5.8–7.2) for Corn. Frost exposure (1.7% of hours at or below freezing) is within the ideal band (0–0.12) for Corn. Soil organic carbon (26.9 g/kg) is within the ideal band (11–28) for Corn. Clay content (32.6%) is within the ideal band (12–38) for Corn. Relative humidity (71.0%) is within the ideal band (45–75) for Corn. Wind speed (7.0 km/h) is within the ideal band (2–16) for Corn. Severe-weather stress (0.0% of hours with severe weather codes) is within the ideal band (0–0.07) for Corn.

What is acceptable but not ideal: Rainfall is 1280 mm (annualized), above the ideal range (450–900) but still inside acceptable bounds (250–1300), which can add stress or management needs.

What works against the site: Mean temperature is 6.6 °C, outside the viable range (10–35) for Corn (ideal 18–27). This heavily penalizes suitability.

Data limitations: no values for Reference evapotranspiration (ET0), Surface soil moisture, Sand content. Those gaps reduce confidence and apply a scoring penalty.

score_explanation: Score combines weather and soil fit against crop-specific ideal ranges, then applies calibrated penalties for missing features and hard-threshold violations. Score bands: poor (<40), fair (40-59), good (60-79), excellent (80-100).
